tips.title: Create a Dedicated Workspace
tips.description: Set up a quiet, organized area for work to maintain focus and productivity.

tips.title: Stick to a Schedule
tips.description: Maintain regular work hours to create boundaries between work and personal life.

tips.title: Minimize Distractions

checklist.title: Get Started Checklist
items: Sign up for remote job alerts

items.question: Are work-from-home jobs legit?
items.answer: Yes! Many legitimate companies hire remote workers. Always research companies and avoid anything asking for upfront payment.

items.question: What equipment do I need to work from home?
items.answer: At minimum: a reliable computer, high-speed internet, and a quiet workspace. Some jobs may require specific software or equipment.

items.question: How do I find remote jobs?
items.answer: Use remote job boards like FlexJobs, We Work Remotely, and Remote.co. Also check company career pages directly.

items.question: Can I work from home without experience?
items.answer: Yes! Entry-level remote jobs exist in customer service, data entry, virtual assisting, and more.

items.question: How much can I earn working from home?
items.answer: Earnings vary widely. Entry-level positions start around $15-25/hour, while skilled roles can earn $50-100+/hour.

items.question: Do remote jobs pay less than office jobs?
items.answer: Not necessarily. Many remote jobs pay competitive salaries. Some even pay more due to reduced overhead costs.

items.question: How do I avoid work-from-home scams?
items.answer: Never pay for a job opportunity, research the company, check reviews, and trust your instincts. Legitimate jobs don't ask for upfront payment.

items.question: Can I work from home outside the country?
items.answer: It depends on the employer. Some allow international work, others require you to be in a specific country. Always check with the employer first.
